C[mkjackson83: There were so many places I wanted to play at this point, but it seemed essential to keep my bottom stones connected
C[mkjackson83: This seems a good response to me
C[mkjackson83: Dividing your stones seemed the best thing to do - already I was wondering if those on the right could be killed
C[mkjackson83: G7 here would be better - it would be keeping your stones safe as well as attacking my top
C[mkjackson83: I considered C6 or E6, but decided I was far too short of liberties on D4, and couldn't risk you killing it
C[mkjackson83: My top is looking weak here, but your centre stones are fragile too.
A less aggressive move like E6 would probably have been better for you than E7 - it would allow me to live easily at the top, but would connect all your stones to life
C[mkjackson83: You had no choice about E6
C[mkjackson83: Killing your right is now my focus
C[mkjackson83: This doesn't help you much - cutting at F4 instead would have left at least some potential to attack E4 or F5

C[mkjackson83: I wasn't sure if this was necessary, but I thought that even if you make some gains around the top and the bottom-left, I would still be ahead by keeping my stones alive and ensuring your right was dead
C[mkjackson83: I was getting a bit worried at this point to be honest, my stones were very thin
C[mkjackson83: This was a relief to me - I was happy to lose E5 for the sake of making use of G6. Something like H6 would have put me in more trouble
C[mkjackson83: I think here you would have done better to play H4 - I then wouldn't be able to make any eyes on the right, and you could kill H6 before I killed G8
C[mkjackson83: From here the game was basically over
GN[9x9 ladder challenge: mkjackson83 vs tethys]
C[mkjackson83: I think this was your key mistake...
C[mkjackson83: Pushing through is the obvious thing for me to do...
C[mkjackson83: By this point your stones were very thin, and I felt that I had the upper hand already
C[mkjackson83: Your last few moves were ok.
Hopefully you realised at this point that I wasn't going to let you connect your 2 groups
C[mkjackson83: Your bottom group was so thin - so many cutting points - that I knew I could damage it.
I was expecting you to answer with F2, after which I'd take the bottom left corner with C2 (then wE1 bB2 or similar)
C[mkjackson83: After you decided to keep the corner, this was a no-brainer for me: eat a couple of stones, keep your groups separated etc.
C[mkjackson83: This was a critical mistake - A2 would have made life comfortably, but B2 leaves a snapback attack open at A2
tethys: Yes, I'd played through a few possibilities and thought it was a defence that worked, but that's because I hadn't spotted the snapback.
C[mkjackson83: After this I think B3 would have given you at least a ko for life
tethys: Possibly yes, but I didn't think I had enough threats elsewhere on the board for it to be worthwhile.

C[msambol: I feel like I'm taking my life in my hands jumping in here, but I guess I've got to give it a shot :)

C[msambol: How do you learn to spot invasions like that? It would never have occurred to me to play there. Obviously I see the power now that I have to deal with it, but ... how do you learn to spot those?
C[mkjackson83: You're talking about A17? It's a pretty common move, called a monkey jump
mkjackson83: so you should already be nervous about S12 :)

C[msambol: When I first got interested in Go a few years ago I read a lot and was full of theoretical knowledge, but had no one to play. When I started playing online I was awful, and pretty quickly realized I needed to stop reading so much and play more. Now I've got a respectable number of games under my belt and have forgotten most of the book knowledge
C[msambol: I don't think there's any way out which gives you at least the bottom half of the board. Thanks for the game. I'd greatly appreciate any review comments you can offer.
mkjackson83: Thank you too, I'd be happy to review
mkjackson83: I've started a review session - are you able to see it? I'll continue when I have time
mkjackson83: I'd be interested to read your comments too
